text: Make the repository surface understandable for new users
description: Add and maintain clear README files, onboarding documentation, package role descriptions, and explanations of what Aimparency is, how it runs locally, and how .bowman data works. The repository should make sense to a first-time visitor without private context.

reflections.context: Make the repository surface understandable for new users by ensuring all documentation is complete and accurate for open source release.
reflections.outcome: All three supporting documentation aims were completed: root README expanded with quickstart and .bowman workflow, package-level READMEs added for backend/broker/shared, and git workflow documented. The repository now has comprehensive onboarding documentation.
reflections.effectiveness: Delegating to sub-aims provided clear, actionable chunks that were completed independently. Marking the parent done once all children were complete maintains consistency in the aim graph.
reflections.lesson: When all supporting aims are marked done, the parent aim should be updated to reflect completion. This keeps the aim graph accurate and prevents stale 'open' status on effectively complete work.
reflections.pattern: This follows standard hierarchical task completion: verify all children are done, then mark parent done with a summary comment.

status.comment: All supporting documentation aims completed: root README expanded, package-level READMEs added for core packages, and .bowman git workflow documented.
